title: artemisia sacrorum var. iwayomogi
intro: Artemisia sacrorum var. iwayomogi is a traditional plant species native to Japan, commonly known as 'iwayomogi' in Japanese, belonging to the Asteraceae family and closely related to the wormwood plant. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. In Japan, iwayomogi is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in herbal supplements, to support overall health and well-being.
